w:r.w:t: Power-First AI Infrastructure at Gigawatt Scale
Delivering hyperscale capacity with speed and certainty
Strategic AI / HPC solutions at speed
Rapidly evolving landscape driven by AI, with the primacy of powe
Dedicated to hyperscale and AI workloads
3+ GW of power capacity across the U.S.
Power capacity secured ahead of land acquisition
Powered land, turnkey shell, build-to-suit, and wholesale lease structures
Single point of accountability across land, power, fibre, permitting, design, and delivery
U.S. hyperscale demand projected to exceed 134 GW by 2030
AI training and inference driving 50–100% annual growth
Traditional 5–7+ year development cycles are no longer viable
long-term power contracts with grid stability
Dark fibre redundancy and latency-optimised locations
Established and optimised specifically to solve this challenge - at speed
Applications, use cases, and demand signals are already material and accelerating
Compute is critical, but power availability ultimately determines capacity
Natural gas remains the only currently scalable energy source at speed
requiring deep understanding of access and delivery
SMRs and alternative solutions remain 7–10 years from meaningful impact
A rapidly evolving sector requiring cross-disciplinary expertise and execution capability
leader with 25+ years’ experience across energy, infrastructure,
and advanced technology, with a focus on large-scale development,
Deep expertise in power, energy systems, and complex infrastructure delivery across private equity, advisory, and operating roles
track record advising governments and leading large-scale projects at the intersection of energy, national security, and technology
Founded in 2025 by experts across energy, power, and AI, including experience from hyperscale, real estate, energy, and investment banking
across power origination, real estate, engineering, construction, project management, government relations, and finance
Supported by a partner network of 40
(a) 275+ MW data centre opportunities across 1 GW+ campuses for Tier-1 cloud and AI operators, including U.S. Government (DoW)
(b) Smaller rapid stand-up opportunities: 10–100 MW in key MSAs
for clients requiring comprehensive solutions, delivered at speed
Secure power and transmission capacity upfront (multi-year processes condensed into months)
Acquire contiguous land within the exact substation footprint
Provide fully energised, entitled, fibre-ready powered land from day one
Prioritise sites with gas infrastructure and availability for behind-the-meter (BTM) builds
24–36 months from LOI to 400 kV energisation
Up to 1,000 MW on day one, expandable to 3+ GW
No comparable platform in the market matches this combination of speed and scale
Up to 150 kW per rack standard; engineered for 300+ kW liquid-cooled deployments
Multiple architectures including air, indirect evaporative, direct liquid, and immersion-ready solutions
Campus-level microgrid and storage options (4–48-hour backup)
Dual 400 kV (or local equivalent) feeds with diverse substations
Client builds on fully powered and entitled land
Energised building with full MEP delivered
Fully integrated design, build, and operate solution
Long-term capacity contracts at campus scale
Expansion rights secured from day one
Single master agreement across multiple campuses globally (where applicable)
One partner, one contract, delivered at global scale
Ongoing discussions with hyperscale leaders, technology partners, and U.S. Government clients
global infrastructure funds, pension capital, and U.S. Government-aligned funding sources
across power providers and grid infrastructure
Designed from the ground up for 100+ kW workloads
One platform delivering global capacity solutions
Let’s power the next generation of AI infrastructure
Andrew Whitney | Chief Executive Officer | andrew@hercules-development.com | +14156025007
Understanding the Range of Outcomes, Then Positioning Accordingly
Bull and bear cases remain uncertain at this
The only certainty is rapid and continuous change
Applying “knowns and unknowns” to navigate an evolving landscape
Energy and power as core pillars
Cross-disciplinary capability as the source of sustained competitive advantage
$800B in market value impacted as downside scenarios begin to price in
AI improving across multiple domains simultaneously
AI positioned as a potential destabilising force across key sectors
Rising layoffs → reduced consumption → increased automation → further displacement
Housing and mortgage markets under pressure
Financial services exposed to asset repricing and liquidity stress
Potential for increased taxation on compute and introduction of UBI frameworks
Simultaneous narratives of labour displacement alongside labour shortages requiring immigration
AI-driven cost reduction across industries drives increased consumption, similar to historical declines in compute costs
increased efficiency leads to higher overall consumption
AI agents optimise price discovery and execution, increasing usage across services and platforms
Rapid growth in digital transaction layers and infrastructure
Significant expansion of domestic manufacturing and infrastructure capacity
Long-term demand for energy, materials, and compute across multiple sectors
AI lowers barriers to entry, enabling large-scale creation of new businesses and platforms
Emergence of new category leaders over time
Rebalancing across software and AI ecosystems as trust, governance, and adoption mature
Careful policy calibration required around compute and global competitiveness
